questions.question: Who recently decided not to continue as the head coach for the Belgium national football team after failing to reach an agreement on a contract extension?
questions.explanation: Rudi Garcia recently decided not to continue as the head coach for the Belgium national football team. He failed to reach an agreement with the country’s soccer federation on a contract extension after leading the team to the World Cup quarterfinals.
questions.background: The coaching position for national football teams often sees changes based on performance and contract negotiations. Belgium's national team, known as the 'Red Devils', is one of the top-ranked teams globally.
questions.examRelevance: Rudi Garcia = former Belgium national football team coach.

source.title: Rudi Garcia is not continuing as Belgium coach after reaching World Cup quarterfinals

questions.question: Which player recently completed a British record move to Chelsea Football Club?
questions.explanation: Morgan Rogers recently completed a British record move to Chelsea Football Club. This transfer follows other significant signings in the British football market.
questions.background: Football transfer records are frequently broken due to the increasing commercialization of the sport. British record moves signify major investments by clubs to acquire top talent.
questions.examRelevance: Morgan Rogers = British record move to Chelsea FC.

source.title: Aston Villa signs João Gomes as star Morgan Rogers completes British record move to Chelsea

questions.question: Johan Manzambi was recently signed by Aston Villa for more than 50 million pounds ($67 million) from which club?

questions.background: Player transfers in top-tier football often involve substantial fees, especially for players who have performed well in major international tournaments like the World Cup. Aston Villa's acquisition reflects their ambition to strengthen their squad.

source.title: Aston Villa signs João Gomes as star Morgan Rogers completes British record move to Chelsea

questions.question: Kevin Keegan, a renowned England soccer star and former player/coach for Newcastle, passed away recently at what age?
questions.explanation: Kevin Keegan, a celebrated England soccer star who also served as a player and coach for Newcastle, passed away at the age of 75. His death was announced on July 20 by his former club.
questions.background: Kevin Keegan was a prominent figure in English football, known for his celebrity status that transcended the game. He had revealed last month that he was battling stage four cancer.

questions.question: The Supreme Court recently proposed a Special Investigation Team (SIT) probe into allegations of donation embezzlement related to which trust?
options: Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court recently proposed a Special Investigation Team (SIT) probe into allegations of donation embezzlement concerning the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust. The bench directed petitioners not to politicize the case and listed it for further orders on July 27.
questions.background: The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust is responsible for the construction and management of the Ram Temple in Ayodhya. Allegations of financial irregularities often attract public and judicial scrutiny, especially for high-profile religious projects.
questions.examRelevance: Supreme Court = proposed SIT probe into Ram Temple donation embezzlement.

source.title: Supreme Court proposes SIT probe into Ram Temple case

questions.question: Which Indian U-19 cricketer recently scored a century, putting India U-19 in command during the 2nd Youth Test?
questions.explanation: Kushagra Ojha, an Indian U-19 cricketer, recently scored a century (a ton) in the 2nd Youth Test. His performance helped India U-19 establish a strong position with 253 for three in 65 overs before play was called off due to bad light.
questions.background: Youth Test matches are crucial for identifying and nurturing future cricketing talent. Performances in these games often serve as a stepping stone for young players to advance to higher levels of the sport.
questions.examRelevance: Kushagra Ojha = scored century in 2nd Youth Test for India U-19.

source.title: 2nd Youth Test: Kushagra Ojha''s ton puts India U-19 in command

questions.question: A recent landslide in Jammu and Kashmir, which tragically claimed seven lives, occurred at which location?
questions.explanation: A tragic landslide in Jammu and Kashmir recently claimed seven lives after hitting a mud house at Domail Dhok. The J&K Police, along with other agencies, actively participated in the rescue operations.
questions.background: Landslides are a recurring natural disaster in the hilly regions of Jammu and Kashmir, often exacerbated by heavy rainfall during monsoon seasons. These incidents frequently lead to loss of life and property.

source.title: 7 killed in landslide in Jammu and Kashmir as rain toll hits 21

questions.question: The recent Monsoon Session of Parliament saw Opposition MPs raising concerns primarily over which two issues?
options: NEET paper leak and Ram Temple donation theft
questions.explanation: The Monsoon Session of Parliament began with stormy protests, as Opposition MPs raised concerns over the NEET paper leak and allegations of Ram Temple donation theft. These issues led to repeated disruptions and the adjournment of the Lok Sabha.
questions.background: Parliamentary sessions often begin with protests and disruptions as opposition parties use the platform to highlight pressing national issues and hold the government accountable. The NEET paper leak and Ram Temple donation allegations are currently significant public concerns.

questions.question: Which major US stock index recently edged down due to investors focusing on Middle East de-escalation and awaiting earnings reports from major technology companies?

questions.question: How many Indian seafarers were recently killed in an attack on a ship leaving Ukraine's Odessa port?

questions.question: Which organization recently led a Parliament march in Delhi that resulted in a crackdown, with several protesters hospitalized and leaders citing 'brutality'?
questions.explanation: The Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) recently organized a Parliament march in Delhi, which faced a police crackdown involving tear gas and lathi-charges. Scores of protesters were hospitalized with injuries, and leaders like Sonam Wangchuk criticized the 'brutality' of the response.
questions.background: Protests in Delhi, especially those targeting Parliament, often lead to significant security deployment and clashes. The CJP protest saw large crowds, predominantly students and young people, reaching the Parliament's doorstep despite multiple layers of barricades and an internet shutdown.
questions.examRelevance: Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) = led Parliament march in Delhi, faced crackdown.

source.title: Several hurt in crackdown on CJP Parliament march

questions.question: Which institution recently introduced low-cost technology to farmers for producing biochar to improve soil health and provided training for its use?
questions.explanation: The University of Agricultural Sciences, Bengaluru (UAS-B), recently introduced low-cost technology to farmers for the production of biochar. They also trained farmers on how to use this biochar to improve soil health.
questions.background: Biochar is a charcoal-like material produced from biomass pyrolysis, known for its ability to enhance soil fertility, water retention, and carbon sequestration. Promoting such sustainable agricultural practices is crucial for environmental conservation and improving farm productivity.
questions.examRelevance: UAS-B = introduced low-cost biochar technology to farmers.

questions.question: What is the recent official stance from India regarding the Indus Waters Treaty, as stated by a government official?
options: It will be fully implemented as is
It will not remain operational in its present form
questions.explanation: An Indian official recently stated that the Indus Waters Treaty will not remain operational in its present form. India had placed the treaty in 'abeyance' following the Pahalgam terror attack, citing Pakistan's continued sponsorship of terrorists.
questions.background: The Indus Waters Treaty, signed in 1960 between India and Pakistan, governs the sharing of the Indus River system. India's latest stance indicates a potential renegotiation or modification of the treaty, reflecting strained bilateral relations.
questions.examRelevance: Indus Waters Treaty = will not remain operational in its present form; placed in abeyance after Pahalgam terror attack.
